original_hypothesis.title: Kernel ChangeExecutor/ChangeAdmin to address(0) Permanently Bricks Protocol Governance
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Kernel.executeAction with Actions.ChangeExecutor or Actions.ChangeAdmin directly sets executor or admin to the target address without validating that target != address(0). If executor is set to address(0), no further module installations, policy activations, or kernel migrations can occur. If admin is set to address(0), no roles can be granted or revoked. This permanently bricks all protocol governance.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Unlike InstallModule and ActivatePolicy which call ensureContract(target_), ChangeExecutor and ChangeAdmin have no validation on the target address. A single accidental or malicious transaction irreversibly destroys protocol governance.

proof_of_signal.pattern: ChangeExecutor: executor = target_ with no address(0) check; ChangeAdmin: admin = target_ with no address(0) check

preconditions: Executor calls executeAction(ChangeExecutor, address(0)) or executeAction(ChangeAdmin, address(0))
No external validation prevents zero-address target

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Set executor or admin to address(0) to permanently disable governance
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Protocol cannot upgrade, add policies, or change fees - all value frozen in current state

economic_questions: What is the total value managed by the protocol that would be affected?
Can active rentals still be stopped if governance is bricked?
validation_requirements: Call executeAction(ChangeExecutor, address(0)) and verify executor == address(0)
Attempt any subsequent executeAction and verify it reverts

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Cannot upgrade, change fees, or manage whitelists

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if executor address is multisig with proper safeguards

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
